---
title: "One-Way ANOVA"
author: Zheng Shi
format: revealjs
---

## Motivation

We want to test whether several treatment means are equal.

Example:

- Control
- Treatment A
- Treatment B

---

## Statistical Model

The one-way ANOVA model is

$$
y_{ij} = \mu + \tau_i + \epsilon_{ij}
$$

where

- $\tau_i$ = treatment effect
- $\epsilon_{ij} \sim N(0,\sigma^2)$

---

## Hypothesis

Null hypothesis

$$
H_0: \tau_1 = \tau_2 = \cdots = \tau_a = 0
$$

Alternative:

At least one treatment differs.

---

## F Statistic

$$
F = \frac{SS_{treatment}/(a-1)}{SS_E/(N-a)}
$$

Large \(F\) suggests treatment effects.

---

## Conclusion

ANOVA partitions variation:

- Between treatments
- Within treatments
